original_abstract: Originally developed for natural language processing, the self-attention mechanism has recently achieved remarkable success across various computer vision domains. However, directly applying self-attention to images presents three significant hurdles: first, flattening images into 1D sequences overlooks their inherent 2D spatial structures; second, the quadratic computational complexity is prohibitive for high-resolution inputs; and third, standard self-attention primarily focuses on spatial adaptability while neglecting channel-wise dynamics. In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention module designed to harness long-range correlations and self-adaptivity while circumventing these limitations. Building on this, we develop the Visual Attention Network (VAN), a straightforward yet powerful architecture. Despite its simplicity, VAN consistently matches or outperforms established convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of tasks, including image classification, object detection, and various forms of segmentation. Notably, the VAN-B6 variant achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet and sets a new state-of-the-art for panoptic segmentation with 58.2 PQ. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly outperforms Swin-T on the ADE20K semantic segmentation benchmark (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and the COCO object detection dataset (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP). Our work offers a fresh perspective on attention mechanisms and provides a robust, simple baseline for the vision community. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.
humanized_abstract: Self-attention has proven to be successful in a variety of computer vision tasks after it was originally proposed for natural language processing applications. Although a self-attention based approach for images provides valuable insights, it has some drawbacks. These include losing spatial information after transforming an image to a 1D sequence, high computational cost due to quadratic operations (especially when dealing with high resolutions) and concentrating on spatial adaptation while leaving channel-wise interaction ignored. This paper introduces Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a linear attention module for exploiting long range relationships and self-adaptation while addressing all of the drawbacks mentioned above. We use LKA for designing the Visual Attention Network (VAN) with excellent performances on a variety of benchmarks. VAN consists of an unusually simple structure that, while having even fewer parameters than many state-of-the-art CNNs and ViTs, often achieves or even surpasses them for image classification, object detection and various semantic segmentation tasks such as ImageNet, COCO, PASCAL VOC, ADE20K, PanopticFCN, etc. Specifically, the VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet and even sets a new state-of-the-art for panoptic segmentation with a score of 58.2 PQ. The VAN-B2 surpasses the state-of-the-art Swin-T on the ADE20K semantic segmentation by 4pt (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and the COCO object detection by 2pt (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP). The experiments here offer a new way of understanding the attention, and meanwhile the VAN can be treated as an easily-accessible and robust baseline for the vision community. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.

document.output: Self-attention has proven to be successful in a variety of computer vision tasks after it was originally proposed for natural language processing applications. Although a self-attention based approach for images provides valuable insights, it has some drawbacks. These include losing spatial information after transforming an image to a 1D sequence, high computational cost due to quadratic operations (especially when dealing with high resolutions) and concentrating on spatial adaptation while leaving channel-wise interaction ignored. This paper introduces Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a linear attention module for exploiting long range relationships and self-adaptation while addressing all of the drawbacks mentioned above. We use LKA for designing the Visual Attention Network (VAN) with excellent performances on a variety of benchmarks. VAN consists of an unusually simple structure that, while having even fewer parameters than many state-of-the-art CNNs and ViTs, often achieves or even surpasses them for image classification, object detection and various semantic segmentation tasks such as ImageNet, COCO, PASCAL VOC, ADE20K, PanopticFCN, etc. Specifically, the VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet and even sets a new state-of-the-art for panoptic segmentation with a score of 58.2 PQ. The VAN-B2 surpasses the state-of-the-art Swin-T on the ADE20K semantic segmentation by 4pt (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and the COCO object detection by 2pt (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP). The experiments here offer a new way of understanding the attention, and meanwhile the VAN can be treated as an easily-accessible and robust baseline for the vision community. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.
